info.title: Rapidata Campaign API
info.description: The API for the Rapidata Campaign service

get.summary: Returns the current state of the in-memory campaign cache.
Returns the current state of the in-memory default user score cache.

post.summary: Creates a program campaign from the given stream program.
post.description:
The program is validated before the campaign is persisted; a malformed graph
 (missing root, dangling references, cycles, unreachable nodes) is rejected. The
 campaign starts in the Created status; move it to Active to start serving sessions.

requestBody.description: The campaign and its stream program.

get.summary: Returns an attributed view of the aggregated boosting profile.
Returns the current boost status including active and inactive campaigns.
Retrieves the details of a specific campaign.

parameters.name: campaignId
parameters.description: The ID of the campaign.

parameters.name: campaignId
parameters.description: The identifier of the campaign to update.

get.summary: Returns the fast bid multiplier currently applied to an external booster campaign.
get.description:
Returns the downstream system's default (1.0) if the multiplier has never been set
 for the campaign.

parameters.name: externalCampaignId
parameters.description: The external booster campaign id to read.

put.summary: Sets the fast bid multiplier for a single external booster campaign to an arbitrary value.
put.description:
Bypasses the level→multiplier mapping. Intended for live testing and one-off
 operational tweaks. Value must be in [0, 50]; 0 effectively pauses delivery on the
 campaign without changing its status. The response reports the multiplier the
 downstream system confirms is applied.

parameters.name: externalCampaignId
parameters.description: The external booster campaign id to update.

get.summary:
Exercises the rapid-bag pipeline with a synthetic user profile so the health of
 the campaign monitoring path can be probed externally.

parameters.name: campaignId
parameters.description: The identifier of the campaign to pause.

post.summary:
Triggers a preheat pass for the boost subsystem so warm caches are ready before
 traffic spikes.

get.summary: Queries campaigns with optional filtering, sorting, and pagination.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The 1-based page index.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: The number of items per page.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort fields. Prefix with - for descending order (e.g. -created_at).

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Filter by id.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Filter by name.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by status.

parameters.name: priority
parameters.description: Filter by priority.

parameters.name: owner_mail
parameters.description: Filter by owner_mail.

parameters.name: created_at
parameters.description: Filter by created_at.

parameters.name: logic
parameters.description: How to combine the field filters: "and" (default) requires every filter to match, "or" requires any of them to match.

parameters.name: campaignId
parameters.description: The identifier of the campaign to resume.

put.summary: Sets or clears the manual global boost level.

requestBody.description: The manual global boost level to apply. Null clears the manual override.

put.description:
The program is validated before it is persisted; a malformed graph is rejected.
 The program's version is bumped and republished — running sessions pick up the new
 program on their next response.

parameters.name: campaignId
parameters.description: The identifier of the program campaign.

get.summary: Queries the cached external audiences available for targeting.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The 1-based page index.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: The number of items per page.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort fields. Prefix with - for descending order (e.g. -created_at).

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Filter by id.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Filter by name.

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: Filter by count.

parameters.name: logic
parameters.description: How to combine the field filters: "and" (default) requires every filter to match, "or" requires any of them to match.

post.summary: Enqueues a background job that synchronises external audiences with the cache.

post.description:
The pattern must be an exact email (user@domain.com) or a wildcard domain
 (*@domain.com), and at least one of the override values must be set.

parameters.name: ownerTierOverrideId
parameters.description: The id of the override to delete.

put.description:
The pattern must be an exact email (user@domain.com) or a wildcard domain
 (*@domain.com), and at least one of the override values must be set.

parameters.name: ownerTierOverrideId
parameters.description: The id of the override to update.

requestBody.description: The new pattern and override values.

isManual.description: Whether the manual overwrite should be applied.

isActive.description: Whether the boost is active. Deprecated in favor of Level.

level.description: The boost level. Takes precedence over IsActive when set.

pattern.description:
The owner-email pattern this override applies to: an exact email (user@domain.com)
 or a wildcard domain (*@domain.com). Exact matches win over wildcard matches.

priorityOverride.description: The priority matching owners' jobs are created with. Null for no priority override.

boostOverride.description: The global boost level matching owners' campaigns are created with (0-10). Null for none.

id.description: The id of the created override.

priority.description: The priority weight the campaign competes with for sessions.

rootNodeId.description: The node the program's evaluation enters at.

nodes.description: The program's decision graph, keyed by node id.

maxRapids.description: The session ends after this many counted responses.

maxDurationSeconds.description: The session ends after this many seconds.

campaignId.description: The id of the created campaign.

manualOverrideLevel.description: The manual override level, if manual mode is active.

globalBoost.description: Global boost insight with contributing campaigns.

activeCampaigns.description: The list of active campaign identifiers.

inactiveCampaigns.description: The list of inactive campaign identifiers.

unknownCampaigns.description: The list of unknown campaign identifiers.

id.description: The unique identifier of the campaign.

status.description: The current status of the campaign.

priority.description: The priority level of the campaign.

hasBooster.description: Whether the campaign has a booster.

boostLevel.description:
The campaign's effective boost level (0-10). 0 when no boost is active.
 Lets clients render and edit the level without unpacking the boosting profile.

requiresBooster.description: Whether the campaign requires a booster.

filters.description: The targeting filters for this campaign.

selections.description: The rapid selections for this campaign.

featureFlags.description: The feature flags configured for this campaign.

ownerMail.description: The email of the campaign owner.

organizationId.description: The id of the organization that owns the entity.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the campaign was created.

count.description: The total number of cached campaigns.

campaigns.description: The list of cached campaign summaries.

externalCampaignId.description: The external campaign id reported by the downstream system.

bidMultiplierValue.description: The current bid multiplier reported for the campaign.

count.description: The total number of cached user scores.

id.description: The unique identifier of the campaign.

status.description: The current status of the campaign.

priority.description: The priority level of the campaign.

hasBooster.description: Whether the campaign has a booster.

boostLevel.description:
The campaign's effective boost level (0-10). 0 when no boost is active.
 Lets clients render and edit the level without unpacking the boosting profile.

boostingControlMode.description:
How the campaign's boost is controlled — Manual (user-editable) or Automatic
 (managed by an owning service; boost edits are rejected by the API).

requiresBooster.description: Whether the campaign requires a booster.

ownerMail.description: The email of the campaign owner.

organizationId.description: The id of the organization that owns the entity.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the campaign was created.

name.description: The name of the external audience.

count.description: The number of users in the audience.

pattern.description: The owner-email pattern the override applies to.

priorityOverride.description: The priority matching owners' jobs are created with, or null.

boostOverride.description: The global boost level matching owners' campaigns are created with, or null.

ownerMail.description: The email of the admin who created the override.

multiplier.description: The bid multiplier to apply. Must be in [0, 50].

externalCampaignId.description: The external campaign id reported by the downstream system.

appliedMultiplier.description: The bid multiplier the downstream system reports as currently applied.

level.description: The global boost level. Null disables manual mode and returns to automatic.

name.description: The new name for the campaign.

priority.description: The new priority value for the campaign.

requiresBooster.description:
Legacy on/off toggle for boosting. Translates server-side to BoostLevel = 3
 when true and BoostLevel = 0 (clear) when false. New clients should send
 BoostLevel directly; this field is kept for backwards compatibility.

boostLevel.description:
The campaign's boost level. Set 1-10 to boost (the audience service decides whether
 the boost manifests as global, distilling, or labeling based on the campaign's
 filters). Set 0 to clear any existing boost. Ignored when BoostingProfile is
 also set (explicit profile wins).

boostingProfile.description:
The boosting profile configuration. Takes precedence over BoostLevel and
 RequiresBooster when set. Prefer BoostLevel — it lets the server derive
 the right profile (including filtered-audience creation) from the campaign's filters.

filters.description: The targeting filters for the campaign.

selections.description: The rapid selections for the campaign.

featureFlags.description: The feature flags for the campaign.

pattern.description:
The owner-email pattern this override applies to: an exact email (user@domain.com)
 or a wildcard domain (*@domain.com).

priorityOverride.description: The priority matching owners' jobs are created with. Null for no priority override.

boostOverride.description: The global boost level matching owners' campaigns are created with (0-10). Null for none.

rootNodeId.description: The node the program's evaluation enters at.

nodes.description: The program's decision graph, keyed by node id.

maxRapids.description: The session ends after this many counted responses.

maxDurationSeconds.description: The session ends after this many seconds.
